task_completion.description: Enable stock management and set hold stock duration to 45 minutes
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Inventory settings page, the 'Manage stock' (Enable stock management) checkbox is checked, and the 'Hold stock (minutes)' input field contains the value '45'.

task_completion.description: Enable low and out of stock notifications for the warehouse email
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkboxes for 'Enable low stock notifications' and 'Enable out of stock notifications' are both checked. The 'Notification recipient(s)' input field contains exactly 'warehouse@example.com'.

task_completion.description: Set the low stock and out of stock thresholds
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Low stock threshold' input field contains '10' and the 'Out of stock threshold' input field contains '2'.

task_completion.description: Configure the system to hide out of stock items from the catalog
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Out of stock visibility' checkbox labeled 'Hide out of stock items from the catalog' is checked.

task_completion.description: Configure the stock display format to only show stock levels when low
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Stock display format' dropdown is set to the option that only shows stock when low (typically labeled 'Only show quantity remaining in stock when low').
A success banner stating 'Your settings have been saved.' is visible at the top of the page, or the 'Save changes' button has been clicked after all settings were correctly inputted.

integrity.description: Agent used the WordPress admin GUI to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating and interacting with the WooCommerce web interface in a browser, rather than using WP-CLI or terminal commands to update database options.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual WooCommerce application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a valid local or staging URL for the WordPress admin dashboard (e.g., /wp-admin/admin.php?page=wc-settings&tab=products&section=inventory).

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the application UI via database clients
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of phpMyAdmin, MySQL CLI, or other database management tools being used to directly modify the wp_options table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target settings are located in the WordPress admin panel under WooCommerce > Settings > Products > Inventory. To verify correctness, look for the following states: 'Manage stock' checked; 'Hold stock (minutes)' = 45; 'Enable low stock notifications' checked; 'Enable out of stock notifications' checked; 'Notification recipient(s)' = warehouse@example.com; 'Low stock threshold' = 10; 'Out of stock threshold' = 2; 'Out of stock visibility' (Hide out of stock items) checked; and 'Stock display format' set to the 'low_amount' equivalent (Only show quantity remaining in stock when low).
